title: Add Stage 3 regression test suite for canonical Agenda storage and CLI flows
goal: Protect the live Stage 3 Agenda baseline with automated regression coverage over canonical task storage, canonical DAG storage, exact deliverable validation, and the active Agenda CLI command flow.
context: Stage 3 Wave A established canonical Agenda storage for tasks, DAGs, and task logs.
Stage 3 Wave B wired the active Agenda command path through azq agenda build, list, show, and dag.
Wave C should close Stage 3 by turning the live implementation into a stable baseline rather than leaving it as a merely functional prototype.

The Stage 3 build document is stale in places: it still frames older azq task or azq dag behavior and broader start or complete flows, while the live repo now exposes the narrower azq agenda command family.
requirements: Add automated tests for task normalization into the canonical Agenda schema.
Add automated tests for task markdown round-trip behavior so canonical task records can be written and read without loss.
Add automated tests for task id allocation from existing TASK_### files.
Add automated tests for parent-deliverable validation against canonical Formam deliverable files.
Add automated tests for canonical DAG JSON round-trip behavior.
Add automated tests for the main Agenda CLI command flow: agenda build, agenda list, agenda show, and agenda dag.
Verify canonical backlinks from tasks to exactly one parent deliverable and from deliverable-scoped DAG refreshes to the canonical parent goal artifact.
Make the regression suite runnable through the repository's normal test command.
Keep the regression suite centered on the live Stage 3 command surface rather than introducing task execution, task status mutation, or later-stage orchestration behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Stage 3 has a real automated regression suite under tests/.
The highest-risk Agenda storage and CLI paths are covered by automated tests.
The tests prove the canonical file-backed task and DAG model behaves deterministically.
The Stage 3 regression suite passes through the project's standard test command.
suggested_commit_message: add stage 3 regression tests for canonical agenda storage and cli flows

title: Align Stage 3 documentation with the live Agenda baseline
goal: Make the operator-facing documentation describe the live Agenda system accurately, explicitly naming canonical Agenda storage and the current azq agenda command family as the Stage 3 baseline.
context: Agenda is now live through canonical file-backed storage under data/agenda/tasks/, data/agenda/dags/, and data/agenda/logs/.
The main docs still present Agenda as later-stage or not-yet-implemented work, and the Stage 3 build doc still carries stale pre-Wave-A and pre-Wave-B gap language.
Wave C is the Stage 3 evidence-and-closeout wave, so the docs should reflect the live baseline before Domum or repository-wide health flows arrive.
requirements: Refactor README.md so the project overview reflects live Agenda behavior rather than only Stage 1 and Stage 2 loops.
Refactor AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md so bootstrap and baseline instructions describe canonical Agenda storage and the current Agenda command family correctly where Stage 3 is discussed.
Refactor AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_3.md so its language matches the implemented Agenda baseline rather than the earlier pre-implementation gap list and older azq task or azq dag framing.
Refactor AZQ_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md so it no longer claims Agenda is only specified in docs and not yet implemented in code.
Explicitly define data/agenda/tasks/, data/agenda/dags/, and data/agenda/logs/ as the canonical Stage 3 system of record.
Remove or rewrite wording that implies Agenda is still only aspirational or that broader start or complete behavior is already live if the code does not support it.
Keep the language aligned with the repository's existing craft and stage vocabulary.
acceptance_criteria: The main operator-facing docs describe file-backed Agenda tasks and DAGs as the canonical Stage 3 storage model.
The docs no longer imply Agenda is only a future design target.
A new operator can read the docs and understand how Agenda records are stored, inspected, and reached from the current azq agenda command surface.
suggested_commit_message: align stage 3 documentation with live agenda baseline
